hero_title: Vidarbha and Marathwada Dairy Development Project
hero_subtitle: An initiative by the Government of Maharashtra and National Dairy Development Board (NDDB) to strengthen dairy infrastructure and support farmers across all 19 districts in Vidarbha and Marathwada regions.
stats_subtitle: Track the progress of Vidarbha and Marathwada Dairy Development Project scheme applications across Vidarbha and Marathwada regions
about_title: Vidarbha and Marathwada Dairy Development Project
about_mission_desc: Empower farmers through financial assistance, modern animal husbandry practices, and comprehensive veterinary support across Vidarbha and Marathwada regions.
about_vision_desc: To establish Vidarbha and Marathwada as self-reliant dairy hubs with sustainable livelihoods, ensuring economic prosperity and food security for farming families.
about_values_desc: Transparency in operations, farmer-Centric approach, efficient service delivery, fair resource distribution, and commitment to sustainable dairy development.
about_highlights: Joint initiative of Government of Maharashtra and NDDB
Modern animal husbandry practices and equipment support
Digital application platform with transparent tracking
Direct benefit transfer (DBT) to farmer’s bank account
Quality Dairy animals and veterinary care support
Capacity building through farmer training programs
Real-time application tracking and status monitoring
Fair and equitable resource allocation system
scheme_components_subtitle: Comprehensive support to dairy farmers through following components
scheme_animal_induction_title: Distribution of high genetic merit animals (cows-buffaloes) to dairy farmers
scheme_animal_induction_desc: Financial assistance for purchasing high-yielding indigenous or crossbred dairy animals.
scheme_hgm_title: Distribution of HGM (IVF – Embryo) pregnant Heifer to the dairy farmer
scheme_hgm_desc: High Genetic Merit pregnant Heifer support for enhanced dairy productivity and breeding excellence
scheme_hgm_benefits: Financial aid for HGM pregnant Heifer acquisition
scheme_fertility_feed_desc: Specialized nutritional supplements to improve reproductive health and fertility in dairy animals
scheme_fodder_seed_title: Supply of feed supplement to enhance Fat and SNF in milk
scheme_fodder_seed_desc: Quality fodder seed distribution for green and dry fodder cultivation to ensure year-round feed availability
scheme_snf_enhancer_title: Subsidy for cultivating perennial fodder crops
scheme_snf_enhancer_desc: Solids-Not-Fat enhancer supplements to improve milk quality and increase farmer income
scheme_snf_enhancer_benefits: Better price realization for quality milk
scheme_chaff_cutter_title: Distribution of power driven chaff cutter to farmers
scheme_chaff_cutter_desc: Mechanized chaff cutting equipment to process fodder efficiently and reduce labor
scheme_chaff_cutter_benefits: Min. 2Hp single phase 2 blade chaff cutter
Equipped with Copper winding electric motor
scheme_silage_desc: Provision of quality silage for round-the-year nutritious fodder availability
scheme_infertile_treatment_title: Treatment of Infertile cows and buffaloes
scheme_infertile_treatment_desc: Comprehensive veterinary treatment program for infertile animals to restore productivity
scheme_training_title: Training to the farmers on modern dairy practices
scheme_training_desc: Capacity building programs on modern dairy management, animal health, and best practices
authorities_subtitle: Meet the leadership team dedicated to implementing and managing the Vidarbha Marathwada Dairy Development Project
authorities_subtitle1: Meet the leadership team dedicated to implementing and managing the Vidarbha Marathwada Dairy Development Project

1.title: Distribution of high genetic merit animals (cow/buffalo) to dairyfarmers
1.shortDescription: Financial assistance for purchasing high-yielding calved indigenous or crossbred dairy cows
1.fullDescription: Under the Vidarbha and Marathwada Dairy Development Project, 13,400 high milk-yielding cows and buffaloes will be distributed over three years across 19 districts. Each beneficiary receives one cow or buffalo with minimum 8-10 litres daily milk production capacity.
benefits: Digital tracking cow collar and geo-tagging included
eligibilityCriteria: Must possess at least 2 milch animals Registerd in Bharat Pashudhan App
Must have sold milk to Milk Producer organization for minimum 3 months in previous year
Not availed government scheme benefits in past 3 years
Only one member per family
Maximum 5 beneficiaries per village
Milk sale proof (3 months minimum from Milk Producer organization)
Purchase Animal From NDDB or Government Registered Vendor
Verification required in Bharat Pashudhan App

subsidyInfo.details: The Maximum cost per animal: ₹1,00,000 including digital tagging, transportation, and insurance, 50% subsidy of cost or ₹50,000 (whichever is less).

termsAndConditions: Animal mortgaged with Project Director during project period
Mandatory digital tracking collar and geo-tagging
In case of death, beneficiary must purchase replacement animal (no cash compensation)

2.title: Distribution of HGM (IVF – Embryo) pregnant Heifer to the dairy farmer
2.shortDescription: Heifers made pregnant through IVF embryos using Sex sorted semen
2.fullDescription: Embryo transfer using In-Vitro Fertilization (IVF) technology produces high-genetic-quality offspring, increasing future high-yielding cows and buffaloes. Pregnant heifers developed using IVF embryos from reputed institutions like MLDB, BAIF, Godrej Agrovet, and NDS.
eligibilityCriteria: Must possess at least 5 milch animals Registered in Bharat Pashudhan App
Must have knowledge of scientific feeding practices
Willing to carry out breeding only through artificial insemination
Verification by project veterinarians
Maximum cost per heifer(7 month pregnant): ₹2,25,000. 75% subsidy or ₹1,68,750 (whichever is less)

subsidyInfo.details: 75% subsidy or ₹1,68,750 (whichever is less). Average cost per heifer: ₹2,25,000 including IVF process, maintenance, and pregnancy.

3.title: Supply of Fertility Feed
3.shortDescription: Specialized NDDB fertility feed to improve reproductive health and reduce repeat breeding
3.fullDescription: NDDB-developed Fertility Feed improves reproductive efficiency and reduces repeat breeding in cows and buffaloes. Feeding 5 kg daily from 60th to 120th day post-calving,By replacing equal quantity of regular cattle field.
eligibilityCriteria: Must own milch animals
Animals in 60-120 day post-calving period

subsidyInfo.details: 25% subsidy provided via DBT after purchase proof submission.

requiredDocuments: Purchase invoice/receipt from Milk producing organization
3.availability: Fertility feed will be available at Milk Pooling point/ Dairy cooperative society level

4.title: Supply of feed supplement to enhance Fat and SNF in milk
4.shortDescription: NDDB-developed Fat and SNF enhancer improve Fat and SNF (Solids-Not-Fat) levels in milk, which determine acceptance and pricing
4.fullDescription: Feeding 250gm daily from 0th to 90th day post-calving, By replacing equal quantity of regular mineral mixture.
benefits: 25% subsidy on Fat and SNF enhancer
Improves milk Fat and SNF content
Better price realization at collection centres
Animals in post-calving period 0 to 90 days after calving
eligibilityCriteria: Must own milch animals
Regular milk supplier to collection centres

subsidyInfo.details: Cost: ₹4,500 per animal (₹200/kg × 22.5 kg for 90 days). 25% subsidy provided via DBT after purchase proof submission.

5.title: Subsidy for cultivating perennial fodder crops
5.shortDescription: Quality fodder seed distribution for year-round feed availability and drought mitigation
5.fullDescription: To mitigate fodder scarcity during drought, quality fodder seeds and saplings provided for cultivation on 22,000 acres across 19 districts. 100% subsidy support for multi-purpose fodder crop cultivation.
eligibilityCriteria: Must own at least 3-4 milch animals
Minimum 1 acre land with irrigation facility
Should not have availed free fodder seed under district schemes

subsidyInfo.details: 100% subsidy up to ₹6,000 per beneficiary for quality fodder seeds and saplings.

6.title: Distribution of power driven chaff cutter to farmers
6.shortDescription: Power driven chaff cutting machines to reduce fodder wastage and improve digestibility
6.fullDescription: Distribution of 10,000 power driven chaff cutters to reduce wastage of green fodder and improve digestibility. Mechanized equipment makes fodder processing efficient and reduces labor requirements.
benefits: ISI-marked quality equipment to Quality Equipment
eligibilityCriteria: Must own at least 3-4 milch animals
Must have electric connection at own cost
Should not have availed similar benefit in past 5 years
Must purchase ISI-marked chaff cutter with minimum 2 HP motor

subsidyInfo.details: Subsidy via DBT after GST invoice submission, 50% subsidy or ₹15,000 (whichever is less).

requiredDocuments: GST invoice of chaff cutter purchase

7.title: Supply Of Silage
7.shortDescription: Preserved green fodder supply during scarcity to maintain continuous milk production
7.fullDescription: During fodder shortage periods, silage (preserved green fodder) helps maintain continuous milk production. Project provides 5 kg silage per day per animal for three months with subsidy support.
eligibilityCriteria: Must own milch animals
Facing fodder shortage situation
Not availed other district fodder schemes

subsidyInfo.details: ₹3 per kg subsidy on silage. Supply of 5 kg per day per animal for three months. DBT transfer after purchase proof.

8.title: Treatment of Infertile cows and buffaloes
8.shortDescription: Comprehensive veterinary treatment program to restore productivity in infertile animals
8.fullDescription: Based on 20th Livestock Census (2019), 30% of 35.62 lakh breedable cows and buffaloes in 19 districts face temporary infertility. Treatment provided through hormonal therapy and traditional methods at veterinary dispensaries.
eligibilityCriteria: Must own infertile cow/buffalo
Animal should be breedable age
Registered with NDLM

subsidyInfo.details: Service fees as per government norms. Treatment through veterinary dispensaries. Detailed records of treated animals and successful conceptions maintained.

9.title: Training to the farmers on modern dairy practices
9.shortDescription: Two-day training programs on modern dairy management practices and best techniques
9.fullDescription: Comprehensive capacity building programs for 36,000 farmers on modern dairy management, animal health, and best practices through recognized institutions like State Animal Husbandry Department, Veterinary Universities, and Krishi Vigyan Kendras (KVKs), and Progressive farmers.
eligibilityCriteria: Dairy farmer from project areas
Willing to attend 1-day training
Committed to implementing learnings
